prompt: What have these competitors shipped, priced, or announced in the LAST ~45 DAYS (roughly late-April through June 2026): Cyphrex (pricing/feature/positioning changes to its $49/$199/$599 continuous tiers and signed reports), Vanta (any 'AI trust', 'AI agent', or attestation product), Lakera / Check Point (post-acquisition agent-security moves), Drata, SafeBase, Whistic, Conveyor? Include funding rounds. Quote announcements/posts (@handle+date) and cite. For each, state whether the move strengthens or threatens kolm's wedge (fixed-fee, Ed25519-signed, named co-signer, standards-mapped readiness report). Be current and specific; if you can't confirm recency, say so.
content:
**Cyphrex (@Cyphrexio)**: Active promotion of cryptographically signed compliance reports (e.g., for SOC 2, EU AI Act, HIPAA) tied to AI agent identity, scoped authorization, and write-time proofs/records. Multiple posts in early-mid June 2026 emphasize signed records for procurement/audits, agent-specific verified identity (Solana-backed), pre-execution checks, and positioning against shared credentials/logs. Examples: June 8, 2026 post on enterprise deals hinging on "signed record of what each agent was authorized to do"; June 7 posts on EU AI Act fines as "documentation failure" requiring signed identity/scope records at execution time; May 2026 posts contrasting pre/post verified identity scenarios for HIPAA and procurement. No explicit new pricing tiers announced in results (query references $49/$199/$599 continuous tiers as part of positioning), but strong emphasis on signed reports as differentiator. No recent funding noted.[[1]](https://x.com/Cyphrexio/status/2063924495891763398)[[2]](https://x.com/Cyphrexio/status/2063606399846822013)

This directly overlaps and **threatens kolm’s wedge** (fixed-fee signed reports with named co-signer/Ed25519, standards-mapped): Cyphrex is actively marketing very similar cryptographic signing + compliance reporting for regulated AI/agent use cases, potentially undercutting on agent-specific identity/scope features.

**Vanta (@TrustVanta)**: Ongoing AI/agent-focused features and positioning as "Agentic Trust Platform." June 2026 activity includes posts on AI product-building framework ("Trustcraft"—no customer data training, internal dogfooding, eval discipline); Vanta Delivers/NY Trust Tour events; employee/best workplaces recognition. Earlier 2026 updates (e.g., March–May) reference AI Agent enhancements, memory/context, vendor risk, questionnaires, and May 2026 VantaCon UK mentions of Agentic Trust Platform + $150M Series C (recency/confirmation of exact announcement timing mixed in sources). No brand-new attestation product or major pricing shifts confirmed in last ~45 days beyond continued AI/agent emphasis.[[3]](https://x.com/TrustVanta/status/2062942923931443557)[[4]](https://www.vanta.com/resources/vantacon-uk-2026-recap)

**Strengthens kolm’s wedge** modestly (AI/agent focus is adjacent but Vanta remains broader continuous monitoring/automation platform; no direct signed/named co-signer reports highlighted recently).

**Lakera / Check Point**: Acquisition closed/expected Q4 2025 (announced Sep 2025); no new post-acquisition agent-security product shipments or moves confirmed in last ~45 days (2026 mentions are retrospective or general AI security bulletins). No recent funding.[[5]](https://www.checkpoint.com/press-releases/check-point-acquires-lakera-to-deliver-end-to-end-ai-security-for-enterprises/)

No material recent move; **neutral** to kolm’s wedge (older integration into broader security stack).

**Drata (incl. SafeBase)**: SafeBase acquisition announced ~Feb 2025 ($250M deal); February 2026 product recap on new GRC experience, AI automation, etc. (pre-dates window). Trust center incidents noted April–May 2026 (unrelated to new features). No new funding or major shipments confirmed in last ~45 days.[[6]](https://drata.com/blog/product-updates-2026-02)[[7]](https://www.securityweek.com/drata-to-acquire-safebase-in-250-million-deal/)

No recent activity in window; **neutral**.

**Whistic, Conveyor**: No recent announcements, product shipments, pricing changes, or funding rounds confirmed in last ~45 days (or readily surfaced in targeted searches). Older positioning in vendor risk/assessments or compliance tools noted but not current.

**Neutral** (no moves to assess impact).

Overall, only Cyphrex shows clear, specific recent activity directly competitive on signed compliance reports; Vanta continues AI/agent evolution without a direct kolm overlap in the period. Most others lack confirmable recency within the ~late April–June 8, 2026 window.
